contents: From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations.Core.
From HoTT Require Import Basics Types Truncations.Core.
(* Given a group [G], we define the centralizer of an element [g : G] as a subgroup and use this to show that the cyclic subgroup generated by [g] is abelian. *)
(* First we show that the collection of elements that commute with a fixed element [g] is a subgroup. *)

Definition centralizer {G : Group} (g : G)
  := fun h => g * h = h * g.

Definition centralizer_unit {G : Group} (g : G) : centralizer g mon_unit
  := grp_g1_1g _ _ idpath.

Definition centralizer_sgop {G : Group} (g h k : G)
           (p : centralizer g h) (q : centralizer g k)
  : centralizer g (h * k).

goals.conclusion: g * h * k = h * k * g
h * g * k = h * k * g

contents:
Definition centralizer_inverse {G : Group} (g h : G)
           (p : centralizer g h)
  : centralizer g h^.

goals.conclusion: h^ * g * h * h^ = h^ * h * g * h^
h^ * g * h = h^ * h * g

contents:
Instance issubgroup_centralizer {G : Group} (g : G)
  : IsSubgroup (centralizer g).

goals.conclusion: forall x y : G, centralizer g x -> centralizer g y -> centralizer g (x * y)
forall x : G, centralizer g x -> centralizer g x^
forall x y : G, centralizer g x -> centralizer g y -> centralizer g (x * y)
forall x : G, centralizer g x -> centralizer g x^

contents:
Definition centralizer_subgroup {G : Group} (g : G)
  := Build_Subgroup G (centralizer g) _.

Definition centralizer_hom {G H : Group} (f : GroupHomomorphism G H) (g g' : G)
  : centralizer g g' -> centralizer (f g) (f g').

goals.conclusion: centralizer g g' -> centralizer (f g) (f g')
centralizer g g' -> centralizer (f g) (f g')
g * g' = g' * g -> f g * f g' = f g' * f g
f g * f g' = f g' * f g

contents: lhs_V napply grp_homo_op; rhs_V napply grp_homo_op.

Definition centralizer_iso {G H : Group} (f : GroupIsomorphism G H) (g g' : G)
  : centralizer (f g) (f g') -> centralizer g g'.

goals.conclusion: centralizer (f g) (f g') -> centralizer g g'
centralizer (f g) (f g') -> centralizer g g'

contents: (* Now we define cyclic subgroups.  We allow any map [Unit -> G] in this definition, because in applications (such as [Z_commutative]) we have no control over the map. *)
Definition cyclic_subgroup_from_unit {G : Group} (gen : Unit -> G) := subgroup_generated (hfiber gen).

(* When we have a particular element [g] of [G], we could choose the predicate to be [fun h => h = g], but to fit into the above definition, we use [unit_name g], which gives the predicate [fun h => hfiber (unit_name g) h]. *)
Definition cyclic_subgroup {G : Group} (g : G) := cyclic_subgroup_from_unit (unit_name g).

Instance commutative_cyclic_subgroup {G : Group} (gen : Unit -> G)
  : Commutative (@group_sgop (cyclic_subgroup_from_unit gen)).

goals.conclusion: group_sgop h k = group_sgop k h

contents: destruct h as [h H]; cbn in H.

goals.conclusion: group_sgop (h; H) k = group_sgop k (h; H)

contents: destruct k as [k K]; cbn in K.

goals.conclusion: group_sgop (h; H) (k; K) = group_sgop (k; K) (h; H)
group_sgop (h; tr H) (k; tr K) = group_sgop (k; tr K) (h; tr H)

contents: (* It's enough to check equality after including into [G]: *)

goals.conclusion:
subgroup_incl (cyclic_subgroup_from_unit gen)
  (group_sgop (h; tr H) (k; tr K)) =
subgroup_incl (cyclic_subgroup_from_unit gen)
  (group_sgop (k; tr K) (h; tr H))

contents: induction H as [h [[] p]| |h1 h2 H1 H2 IHH1 IHH2].

goals.conclusion: gen tt * k = k * gen tt

contents: induction K as [k [[] q]| |k1 k2 K1 K2 IHK1 IHK2].

goals.conclusion: gen tt * k = k * gen tt
gen tt * (k1 * k2^) = k1 * k2^ * gen tt

hypotheses.type: gen tt * k1 = k1 * gen tt
gen tt * k2 = k2 * gen tt

goals.conclusion: gen tt * k = k * gen tt
gen tt * gen tt = gen tt * gen tt
gen tt * (k1 * k2^) = k1 * k2^ * gen tt

hypotheses.type: gen tt * k1 = k1 * gen tt
gen tt * k2 = k2 * gen tt

contents: srapply (issubgroup_in_op_inv (H:=centralizer k)); unfold centralizer; symmetry; assumption.

Definition abgroup_cyclic_subgroup {G : Group} (g : G) : AbGroup
  := Build_AbGroup (cyclic_subgroup g) _.

(** ** Centralizers of general subsets *)

(** The centralizer of the elements of [G] for which [H] holds. *)

Definition subtype_centralizer {G : Group} (H : G -> Type)
  : G -> Type
  (* Note the order of operations here.  We do this to match the original proofs for the centraliser of an element. *)
  := fun g => merely (forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h g).
(** The centralizer of any subset of [G] is a subgroup of [G]. *)

Instance issubgroup_subtype_centralizer {G : Group} (H : G -> Type)
  : IsSubgroup (subtype_centralizer H).

goals.conclusion:
forall x y : G,
subtype_centralizer H x ->
subtype_centralizer H y -> subtype_centralizer H (x * y)
forall x : G, subtype_centralizer H x -> subtype_centralizer H x^
forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h 1

forall x y : G,
subtype_centralizer H x ->
subtype_centralizer H y -> subtype_centralizer H (x * y)
forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h (x * y)

hypotheses.type: forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h y
forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h x
forall h0 : G, H h0 -> centralizer h0 y
forall h0 : G, H h0 -> centralizer h0 x

goals.conclusion: forall x : G, subtype_centralizer H x -> subtype_centralizer H x^
forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h x^

hypotheses.type: forall h : G, H h -> centralizer h x
forall h0 : G, H h0 -> centralizer h0 x

contents:
Definition subtype_centralizer_subgroup
  {G : Group} (H : G -> Type)
  := Build_Subgroup G (subtype_centralizer H) _.
